changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Satoko's Trap Course (Advanced)"

TextENGNew: It had been a peaceful lunch break.
Thud,@k@|@y clatter,@k@|@y splaaaaaash!!!
``Whaa?! What was that?!``
``I just heard something really loud from the hallway! What was it?@k@vS20/02/440200073. What was it?!``
We rushed out to the hallway... and there, to our amazement, was Coach with an upturned water bucket on his head!
``C-Coach, are you okay?!``
``Taha, hahahaha... Man... you sure got me...@k@vS20/10/441000002. Hello, everyone.``
Coach greeted us with a refreshing composure, as if nothing had happened.
``What's going on, Coach? You don't come to school very much.``
``Well, I do come here once a week as part of my job. It had been quiet here for a while... but I totally let my guard down...``
``Ahahaha... Satoko-chan's little tricks always come when you least expect them...@k@vS20/02/440200075. Will you be okay in those clothes?``
``Oh, I'm fine—I was just thinking of taking a nice bath, so this is actually cooling me off.``
``You sure are calm, Coach. In fact, you never get mad!@k@vS20/03/440300101. It really makes you come off as an adult.``
...She was right.
If it were me, I would have gone tearing around with the bucket still on my head, hunting for the culprit.
His mature refusal to get worked up over every childish prank was truly admirable.
``Listen, Maebara-san. With things like this, changing your perspective just a little can make it either irritating or pleasant.@k@vS20/10/441000006. It's all about how you react to it.``
``...If there's some secret trick to staying so calm after getting hit in the head with a bucket filled with water, then I'm begging you, tell me.``
``For example... Let's say there was this old and traditional family, dating back to the Meiji era.``
``Okay.``
``And let's say they had many maids under their employ.

Being a large house, of course, they have all kinds of maids—from the veteran ones who turned mean and don't want to give up their jobs, to the young ones full of energy!! See, can you imagine that?@k@vS20/10/441000010. Yes...♪
A space of pink, dreamy maids adorned with frills and headbands spread outward with Coach at its center...
``The newer maids, with all their youthful vitality, giving everything they have to please their master! But the older maids won't allow that to happen!
They find fault with everything they do, bullying them and tormenting them all day long!!
Look, there's still so much dust here! Oh... I'm so sorry...@k@vS20/10/441000015. Do it over!@k@vS20/10/441000016. Splash!!
A bucket falls upside-down on the maid! She's soaking wet... and she wants to cry, but still puts on a brave face... haa haa!!@k@vS20/10/441000018. Maaaiiidddsss~~~~♪♪♪!!``
``...Hau~~~☆ I don't get it but it sounds fun~☆@k@vS20/02/440200077. Maybe Rena should be a maid too...♪``
``Ah, geez, stop! You've even infected Rena!@k@vS20/03/440300103. And you, Kei-chan!@k@vS20/03/440300104. Don't go getting lost forever in Coach's world!``
``...If not for this character trait, Coach would be a good person, too.``

``Mgh! One Satoko Houjou-san sighted!@k@vS20/10/441000020. ...I say, Satoko-san, what is this!@k@vS20/10/441000021. I say, look at all this dust!@k@vS20/10/441000022. I-I-I say!!@k@vS20/10/441000023. It's time for you to be punished!``
Coach plucked Satoko up and started giving her a spanking.
``Ahahahahahahaha! Satoko-chan, you're so kyute!@k@vS20/02/440200079. Ah-hahahahaha!``
"...Satoko can orchestrate traps with such brilliant foresight -- yet somehow she can never predict what happens afterward."
``Is it? I think Satoko-chan wanted this to happen. She wanted it!``
``Satoko wanted this outcome? What do you mean by that?``
``When she plays pranks on people like this, they always get mad and chase her around. Don't you think it's like a form of communication?``
``...Sounds like a terrible way to communicate. Don't people normally say 'hello' to each other?``
``Oh, Keiichi-kun, haven't you heard it of before? People who play lots of pranks are actually lonely and want people to pay attention to them.``
Rena smiled a serene, cooling smile and explained with a soft laugh.
changeReason: Original says 'laughing abruptly' while the Japanese (ふっと笑いながら) means 'laughing softly/gently' — the new translation corrects the characterization of the laugh.

TextENGNew: ``That's what I mean. ...If Satoko-chan plays a trick on someone, she wants them to get mad or laugh and chase her.@k@vS20/02/440200084. It's kind of like a kitty biting you softly... Don't you think it's cute...?``
``They will definitely be surprised right here and jump backwards. So if I put it in this spot, it will work for certain!!``
While Satoko expounded on her trap philosophy with evident pride, she was busy converting the mountain into an even more fiendish gauntlet of traps.
If Rena was right -- if Satoko's traps were really just the flip side of wanting attention...
Then the countless traps lying dormant across this mountain were all... crystallizations of that longing.
Alone with Rika-chan... setting up trap after trap after trap throughout the mountains...@k surely waiting all along for someone to stumble into them.
...But nobody needed to come to this mountain in the first place.
...Without a doubt, she and Rika-chan had spent a lot of time imagining how victims caught in their traps would react.
Satoko, torn from her beloved Nii-nii, Satoshi.
...Too stubborn to ever utter the words "please come home."
But the countless traps sleeping on this mountain revealed Satoko's true feelings.
``...Keiichi-san! Are you listening?``
``Oh, sorry about that. What did you say?``
``All you have to do is pound one nail into the tree trunk like this, and it's already a great trap. If you trip up right there and smack your forehead into it... it would really hurt~!``
``A-A human hammer pushing a nail into your forehead... Y-Yeah, that sounds painful...``
``If it was someone really, really evil, this trap could put them to death! I do so look forward to seeing what sort of villain gets caught by it!``

``...Why do you keep glancing at me like that? ...Are you saying you want me to fall for it?@k@vS20/01/440100130. ...I-I could get really badly hurt, you know.@k@vS20/01/440100131. Maybe you shouldn't be using this trap.``
``......Is that so? ...Well, it is true that I can't use traps without love behind them.
...Then I'll just move the nail here so that if the villain really reforms their behavior, they won't get caught by it...``
Satoko immersed herself in adjusting the nail's position.
...Traps born of love, huh?
Maybe Coach had been right after all.@k...Just by changing how you looked at things... everything was different.
If you thought of it that way... perhaps there was humor and warmth to be found even in falling for Satoko's traps.
``All finished!! Now it's absolutely perfect where it is!@k@vS20/04/440400087. What do you think, Keiichi-san?``
``Uh... yeah. It's perfect.@k@vS20/01/440100133. I hope a villain comes along and gets caught by this trap.``
``Ho-ho-ho-ho! No, of course it would be better if that didn't happen.``
...It looked a little painful, but I thought that maybe next time I came here, I wouldn't mind getting caught in it.
